Module 2 | Trail running

BasicsTrail modelsLineupSell it
Module 2

Trail running

Start with the type of trail run

Trail conversations get easier when you ask three things first: how far, what kind of ground, and what the runner wants to feel underfoot. Then the right shoe becomes much clearer.

Trail running basics

Understand what trail running is and how terrain and distance affect shoe choice

Trail running takes place off-road across surfaces like dirt paths, gravel tracks, forest trails, and rocky mountain routes. Because the terrain is less predictable than road running, the right shoe needs to offer more grip, protection, and stability. The terrain types below will help you match each runner to the best option.

Predictable surface, minimal elevation

Smooth terrain

Great for beginners or recovery runs.

  • Cushioned ride
  • Flexible sole
  • Moderate grip
  • Lightweight build

Up to 42 km

Short

Short trail efforts still need grip and confidence, but they do not always demand the most protective or most cushioned option.

Mix of uphill and downhill, moderate technicality

Rolling terrain

Varied foot placement and rhythm changes call for balanced product choices.

  • Versatility
  • Grip plus comfort
  • Mid-range stack and drop
  • Responsive midsole

42 to 80 km

Long

This is where comfort, traction, and stability need to work together so the runner can stay smooth over changing ground.

Steep, unstable, and less predictable

Technical terrain

This is where protection, precision, and confidence become the selling language.

  • Aggressive lug pattern
  • Rock protection
  • Reinforced upper
  • Secure fit

80 km and beyond

Ultra

Ultra distance raises the importance of fatigue management, impact distribution, fit security, and durable grip.

Trail models

The following are key Salomon trail running models and who they are for

Dynamic comfort

Ultra Glide 4, Ultra Flow 2

Dynamic Comfort is best for long distances, comfort-focused runners and everyday trail use.

Dynamic support

Genesis, XA Pro 3D V9

It is best for uneven terrain, stability needs and confidence on trails.

Dynamic speed

Pulsar

This is best for faster runs, racing, lightweight feel.

Grip and mud

Speedcross 6, Speedcross Peak

This is best for muddy, wet or slippery trails.

Versatility and value

Alphaglide

This is best for entry-level trail runners, mixed terrain, value seekers.

Trail lineup

The full trail running range

Click each shoe to reveal more product detail.

Dynamic comfort

Dynamic Comfort is best for long distances, comfort-focused runners and everyday trail use. Let's take a closer look at the Ultra Glide 4 and Ultra Flow 2.

Ultra Glide 4

Made for long-distance comfort

Ease into the Ultra Glide 4 and feel the difference. The latest version of Salomon's most comfortable trail running shoe is lighter, more breathable, and more dynamic underfoot, so the runner can stack up the miles comfortably.

Reveal more

Highlight

1

Engineered Mesh sensiFIT & endoFIT

Light weight, breathable and comfortable, featuring 50% recycled content

2

optiFOAM

Is a super springy and comfortable midsole compound

3

relieveSPHERE

Designed to distribute pressure and absorb repetitive impact underfoot for lasting comfort

4

All Terrain contaGRIP®

Confidence in a wide variety of terrains

Key features

  • Dynamic cushioning
  • Grip
  • Long-lasting comfort
  • This is a great option if comfort is your priority on longer trail runs.

Tech features

Drop

6 mm

41 / 35mm

Weight

280 g

M UK 8.5

250 g

W UK 5.5

Lug

4 mm

Cushion

Maximum

Terrain

Mixed

Fit

Standard

What to say

Lead with Ultra Glide 4 when the customer wants one of the most comfortable trail shoes in the range without losing stability and trust underfoot.

What differentiates the Ultra Glide 4 from the Ultra Glide 3?

The latest version is lighter, more breathable, and more dynamic underfoot for long-distance comfort.

Ultra Flow 2

Made for new trail runners looking for comfort trails

Ultra Flow 2 makes endurance feel effortless. It pairs optiFOAM cushioning, reassuring grip, and a lightweight breathable upper to create a softer, more approachable trail experience.

Reveal more

Highlight

1

3D Mesh & sensiFIT

Technology that wraps your foot like a car’s seatbelt

2

optiFOAM

Is a super springy and comfortable midsole compound

3

Relieve SPHERE

Designed to distribute pressure and absorb repetitive impact underfoot for lasting comfort.

4

All Terrain contaGRIP®

Confidence in a wide variety of terrains

Key features

  • Dynamic cushioning
  • Grip
  • Snug fit
  • This feels comfortable and forgiving, especially if you're new to trail running.

Tech features

Drop

6 mm

34 / 28mm

Weight

273 g

M UK 8.5

233 g

W UK 5.5

Lug

4 mm

Cushion

Maximum

Terrain

Mixed

Fit

Standard

What to say

Use Ultra Flow 2 for new trail runners, road runners trying trail for the first time, or anyone who wants Ultra Glide comfort at a more accessible level.

Dynamic support

It is best for uneven terrain, stability needs and confidence on trails.

Genesis

Made for fun, day-long mountain runs in technical terrain

Genesis is a mountain running shoe that stays comfortable, confidence-boosting, dynamic, and agile at the same time. It is designed for hard-wearing durability and precise movement in tricky terrain.

Reveal more

Highlight

1

Matryx®

Upper Matryx® technology is highly resistant. Seamless, lightweight, foothold, and breathable

2

optiFOAM

Is a super springy and comfortable midsole compound.

3

activeCHASSIS

A chassis for increased guidance, uncompromised agility and fluid freedom of movement

4

All Terrain contaGRIP®

Confidence in a wide variety of terrains

Key features

  • Running performance
  • Resistance
  • Grip
  • This helps keep your foot stable when the ground is uneven.

Tech features

Drop

8 mm

30 / 22mm

Weight

269 g

M UK 8.5

242 g

W UK 8.5

Lug

4.5 mm

Cushion

Maximum

Terrain

Rocky and

mixed terrains

Fit

Standard

What to say

Use Genesis for short or long mountain adventures when the customer wants comfort and stability together, especially on rolling to technical terrain.

XA Pro 3D V9

Made for versatility on technical hikes and runs

XA Pro 3D V9 is the icon for stability, durability, and support in a trail package tough enough for demanding adventure. Originally introduced for trail running, it is now equally trusted for multi-activity use.

Reveal more

Highlight

1

3D mesh welded upper with sensiFIT

For comfort and foothold

2

Large protective toe cap

3

advancedCHASSIS

This chassis provides stability on the rear-foot while keeping the front flexible

4

All Terrain contaGRIP®

Confidence in a wide variety of terrains. Upgraded pattern and compound vs V8

Key features

  • Stability
  • Protection
  • Grip
  • Durability
  • This is ideal if you want a very stable, do-it-all trail shoe.

Tech features

Drop

11 mm

28.7 / 17.3mm

Weight

323 g

M UK 8.5

300 g

W UK 5.5

Lug

3.5 mm

Cushion

Regular

Terrain

Mixed, rocky

Fit

Standard - wide

version available

What to say

Call it the Swiss army knife of shoes. It is the right move when the conversation expands beyond pure running into hike, walk, travel, or all-round adventure.

Dynamic speed

This is best for faster runs, racing, lightweight feel.

Pulsar

Made for your fast(er) daily runs

Pulsar is built to pump up the pace with exceptional propulsion and cushioning. It gives the trail runner a more road-running-like sensation while keeping Salomon grip and stability.

Reveal more

Highlight

1

quickLACE

2

sensiFIT & endoFIT

Cradles the foot from midsole to laces to ensure a secure and snug mid foot support

3

optiFOAM2

Is a super springy and comfortable midsole compound.

4

All Terrain contaGRIP®

Confidence in a wide variety of terrains

Key features

  • Superfoam cushioning
  • Grip
  • Lightweight feel
  • This is best if speed and performance matter most to you.

Tech features

6 mm

29 / 23mm

Weight

M UK 8.5

265 g

W UK 5.5

Lug

4 mm

Cushion

Maximum

Terrain

Mixed

Fit

Standard

What to say

Position Pulsar for runners who want to go faster on rolling terrain and keep the ride feeling lively, light, and more road-inspired.

Grip and mud

This is best for muddy, wet or slippery trails.

Speedcross 6

Made for training with grip and traction

Speedcross 6 stays true to its legendary roots while getting lighter and even more grippy. It is the fast answer for muddy, soft, and uneven ground where traction and confidence matter most.

Reveal more

Highlight

1

quickLACE

2

Ripstop fabric upper

Abrasion resistant (30% recycled materials) with sensiFIT

3

energyCELL+

A midsole compound that provides energy return along with substantial cushioning and durability

4

Mud contaGRIP®

Traction and mud evacuation in muddy, soft or uneven terrain

Key features

  • Grip
  • Fit
  • Comfort
  • If you often run in mud, this gives you excellent grip and control.

Tech features

10 mm

32 / 22mm

Weight

M UK 8.5

262 g

W UK 5.5

Lug

5 mm

Cushion

Regular

Terrain

Muddy

Fit

Standard

What to say

Describe it in one sentence: speed and confidence on muddy trails. This is still the shoe that brought speed to the trails.

Speedcross Peak

Made for easy runs with grip and traction

Speedcross Peak keeps the aggressive outsole and unmistakable Speedcross DNA, but packages it in a more accessible, easier-going proposition for broader trail use.

Reveal more

Highlight

1

quickLACE

2

sensiFIT

Cradles the foot from midsole to laces to ensure a secure and snug mid foot support

3

fuzeFOAM

Soft EVA foam for a smooth yet dynamic stride

4

All Terrain contaGRIP®

Confidence in a wide variety of terrains

Key features

  • Precise fit
  • All-terrain protection
  • Active grip
  • This gives you Speedcross grip with a lighter, more versatile feel.

What to say

Use it when the customer likes the Speedcross story but does not need the most premium or most aggressive version of that idea.

Versatility and value

This is best for entry-level trail runners, mixed terrain, value seekers.

Alphaglide

Made for easy and versatile runs

Alphaglide is a versatile trail shoe that scores high on cushioning, comfort, and terrain adaptability. It is reassuring in grip, broad in use, and easy to recommend.

Reveal more

Highlight

1

sensiFIT

Cradles the foot from midsole to laces to ensure a secure and snug mid foot support

2

fuzeFOAM

Soft EVA foam for a smooth yet dynamic stride

3

All Terrain contaGRIP®

Confidence in a wide variety of terrains

Key features

  • Responsive ride
  • Foothold protection
  • Versatile grip
  • This is a solid, comfortable trail shoe if you want value and versatility.

What to say

Use Alphaglide when the customer wants one reliable trail answer that can handle easy runs, mixed use, and a wide variety of trail moments.

Selling starts

Keep the recommendation simple and easy to say

Listen for the customer clue, name the best place to start, and explain the benefit in plain language.

Tap a customer line to reveal the answer

Start with

Ultra Glide 4

Backup

Ultra Flow 2

Why

Ultra Glide 4 is the premium long-distance comfort answer. Ultra Flow 2 keeps the ride softer, simpler, and more accessible.

Start with

Ultra Flow 2

Backup

Alphaglide

Why

Ultra Flow 2 feels welcoming and cushioned. Alphaglide becomes the better answer when versatility matters more than plushness.

Start with

Genesis

Backup

XA Pro 3D V9

Why

Genesis is the mountain-running answer. XA Pro 3D V9 becomes the better move when the use case broadens into hike or all-round adventure.

Start with

Speedcross 6

Backup

Speedcross Peak

Why

Speedcross 6 is the full legend. Speedcross Peak keeps the same DNA while softening the feel and the price.

Start with

Pulsar

Why

Pulsar is the best answer when the runner wants a faster, more energetic, more road-like trail feel.
